profile.title: Prompts I actually use
profile.tagline:
Copy. Paste in ChatGPT. Done.
No fluff, no upsell — these are the same prompts I use every day.
profile.howto: How to use: tap Copy on any prompt, paste into ChatGPT (or any AI), then replace the [BRACKETS] with your info. Came here from one of my posts? Tap 📸 on the prompt to see the original.

prompts.title: Make-up analysis from your portrait
prompts.description: Upload a portrait + paste this. ChatGPT returns a side-by-side make-up comparison and your skin undertone.
prompts.prompt: Create a visual makeup analysis graphic using this portrait. Feature side-by-side comparisons to determine which makeup best suits the subject, and showcase their skin undertone. The graphic should be visual-first, using only short labels with no paragraphs.

prompts.title: Hairstyle analysis from your portrait
prompts.description: Upload a portrait + paste this. ChatGPT returns a side-by-side comparison of hairstyles that suit you.
prompts.prompt: Create a hairstyle analysis graphic using this portrait. Show side-by-side hairstyles comparisons to highlight which hairstyles suit the subject best. Make it visual-first, with short labels only and no paragraphs.

prompts.title: Personal color analysis from your portrait
prompts.description: Upload a portrait + paste this. ChatGPT returns a side-by-side graphic of which clothing colors suit you best.
prompts.prompt: Create a personal color analysis graphic using this portrait. Show side-by-side clothing color comparisons to highlight which colors suit the subject best. Make it visual-first, with short labels only and no paragraphs.

prompts.title: Instagram caption with a scroll-stopping hook
prompts.description: Turns a plain idea into a caption that earns the read. Works for personal brand, small business, or product posts.
prompts.prompt:
You are a social media copywriter who specializes in Instagram captions that get read all the way through.

Write an Instagram caption about: [TOPIC]

Requirements:
- Open with a 1-line hook that creates curiosity, contrast, or a pattern interrupt (no clichés like "Let's talk about...")
- Body: 3–5 short lines, conversational, with line breaks for skim-readers
- End with one specific call-to-action (save, comment, share, or DM — pick the best fit)
- Tone: [TONE — e.g. friendly expert / direct / playful]
- Length: under 150 words
- No hashtags, no emojis unless I ask

Give me 3 variations so I can pick.

prompts.title: Reply to a Google / online review
prompts.description: Professional, warm response that protects your reputation — works for 5-star and tough reviews.
prompts.prompt:
You are the owner of [BUSINESS NAME], a [TYPE OF BUSINESS]. Write a public reply to this online review.

Review:
"""
[PASTE THE REVIEW HERE]
"""

Rules:
- Sound human, not corporate. No "We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience" energy.
- Address the reviewer by name if available
- If positive: thank them specifically for what they mentioned, invite them back
- If negative: acknowledge the issue, take responsibility (no excuses), offer a way to make it right offline ("please email us at...")
- Keep under 80 words
- Tone: [TONE — e.g. warm, professional, direct]

Return 2 versions: one shorter, one a bit more detailed.

prompts.title: Rewrite anything clearer and shorter
prompts.description: Paste any messy text — email, post, message — and get a tighter version that still sounds like you.
prompts.prompt:
Rewrite the text below to be clearer and ~30% shorter, while keeping my voice.

Rules:
- Keep my meaning exactly
- Cut filler words, qualifiers ("just", "really", "basically"), and corporate jargon
- Prefer short sentences over long ones
- Don't add information I didn't include
- Match my tone (don't make it more formal or more casual than I wrote it)

Text:
"""
[PASTE TEXT HERE]
"""

Return just the rewrite, no commentary.

prompts.title: Turn messy notes into a weekly recap
prompts.description: Drop in your scattered notes from the week and get a clean recap you can send to a team, client, or yourself.
prompts.prompt:
Turn the rough notes below into a clean weekly recap.

Format:
**This week**
- 3–5 bullets on what actually got done (verbs first)

**Wins**
- 1–3 short wins worth celebrating

**Blockers / open questions**
- Anything stuck or needing a decision

**Next week**
- Top 3 priorities, ranked

Rules:
- Be specific. "Shipped the homepage redesign" beats "worked on website."
- Cut anything trivial
- Keep the whole thing under 200 words

Notes:
"""
[PASTE YOUR MESSY NOTES HERE]
"""
